acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All dependencies added to Cargo.toml
- [ ] `beads_rs::core::*` paths work via re-export
- [ ] `beads_rs::api::*` paths work via re-export
- [ ] `beads_rs::daemon::ipc::*` paths work via shim
- [ ] `beads_rs::daemon::{BeadPatch, Filters, ...}` work
- [ ] CLI compiles without changes to command handlers
- [ ] `cargo check` passes for entire workspace
- [ ] `cargo test` passes
- [ ] `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean
title: Phase 4: Re-wire beads-rs daemon with re-export shims

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Checkpoint export is scheduled and produces commits under new refs.
- [ ] Dirty events during export schedule a follow up checkpoint.
- [ ] Legacy sync remains available for migration but does not write new realtime refs.
description:
**Problem**
Phase 6: integrate checkpoint scheduling and git ref wiring. Export and import modules alone are not used until daemon schedules checkpoints and writes the new refs.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.2 (refs), §14 (scheduler), §7.3 (startup flow)
**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/*, src/daemon/scheduler.rs or new checkpoint runtime module, src/daemon/git_worker.rs, src/git/sync.rs (legacy)
design:
**Design**
- Add checkpoint runtime that tracks dirty namespaces and schedules exports with debounce, max interval, and max events.
- Write checkpoint commits to `refs/beads/<store_id>/<group>` and keep `refs/beads/meta` for discovery.
- Ensure only one push in flight per group and coalesce when new events arrive.
- Keep legacy `src/git/sync.rs` as read only migration path.
title: Phase 6: Checkpoint scheduler + git ref wiring

description:
**Problem**
bd-3m5.17 is too large. Split: manager handles outbound peers and reconnection.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §9.1 (manager.rs)
- Refines bd-3m5.17

**Design**
- Implement ReplicationManager in src/daemon/repl/manager.rs
- Manages outbound peer connections from config (replicas.toml)
- Handles reconnect with exponential backoff
- Event fanout to connected sessions
- Namespace scoping based on policy (replicate=anchors/peers/none)

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Manager connects to configured peers
- [ ] Reconnect with backoff on disconnect
- [ ] Events fanned out to eligible sessions
- [ ] Namespace policy respected

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/manager.rs
title: Phase 5: Replication manager + outbound peer lifecycle

description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §5.2.3 says *.wal.tmp files MUST be ignored and SHOULD be deleted if not referenced by wal.sqlite. Current replay ignores .tmp files but never cleans them up.

Evidence:
- src/daemon/wal/replay.rs list_segments only loads *.wal; no cleanup of *.wal.tmp.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] On startup/replay, delete .wal.tmp files not referenced in wal.sqlite segments table.
- [ ] Log deletions for operator visibility.
title: Startup does not clean up orphaned *.wal.tmp segments

description:
**Problem**
Symmetry reduction currently only canonicalizes actor indices; replica IDs embedded in stream keys keep equivalent states distinct. This limits reduction for 3+ replicas.

**Design**
- Introduce a model-local replica key (index) used for stream keys/digests/invariants.
- Keep a mapping Id -> ReplicaId for production ingest/frames; convert only at the model boundary.
- Implement Rewrite<Id> for the model-local replica key / stream key so Representative can rewrite it.
- Ensure no production logic is reimplemented; adapters remain thin.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Symmetry on reduces state space further for >=3 replicas vs current baseline.
- [ ] All invariants still pass.
- [ ] Models still use production ingest adapters only.

**Files**
- beads_stateright_models/examples/repl_core_machine.rs
- (optional) src/model/* adapters if a model-only key type is needed
title: Stateright repl: full symmetry for replica ids

description: Cache-first short-circuit can suppress fresher remote checkpoint discovery. Introduce typed candidate comparison and authoritative selection (freshness/era) so startup cannot ingest stale snapshot by control-flow accident.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit: landed in /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs at jj change 7853dbe0 (bd-g2qm: choose checkpoint authority deterministically). Cache and git checkpoint imports now carry typed authority metadata, newer checkpoints win explicitly, equal timestamps prefer git over cache, and same-timestamp/fresher-git regressions were added. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check; just dylint; cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo test -p beads-daemon --features test-harness; cargo test; cargo xtest. Adversarial review rerun: 2 reviewers, both clean after fixing the equal-timestamp authority gap and boxing the enlarged outcome payload for clippy.

title: P0: Deterministic checkpoint authority selection across cache and git
